Vancouver councillors call for mandatory side guards on city trucks   The motion, expected to be presented to Vancouver city council on Tuesday, comes after the death of 28-year-old UBC student Agustin Beltran. The cyclist collided with a truck at the intersection of Pacific and Hornby Streets on June 29.

Rescue crews locate body of missing man at site of collapsed East Van parking lot   The male employee's body was recovered Friday evening after he was declared deceased at the scene. News reports identify him as Ben Sotelo.

Rare low tides expose rocks that rode glaciers for hundreds of kilometres   The rock most likely travelled — via glacier — hundreds of kilometres from the Coast Mountains north of Vancouver between 10,000 and 20,000 years ago.
Bank of Canada chief sees inflation 'a little over' 8% as soon as next week   Canadian inflation was 7.7% in May, the highest since January 1983.

29 candidates graduate from first RCMP Indigenous training program held since COVID-19 pandemic   There were 29 graduates from the Indigenous Pre-Cadet Training Program, aimed at giving Indigenous people between ages of 19 and 29 a first-hand look into a potential career in policing.

June home sales down 24% from last year, 6% since May: CREA   CREA attributed the drops to financial pressures prospective buyers have experienced as the Bank of Canada has continued to hike its key interest rate.
House of Commons industry committee to discuss investigating Rogers outage   The outage lasted more than 15 hours, affecting mobile and internet users.
Deadline for Rogers, Shaw, Quebecor to reach definitive agreement on Freedom sale   Last month, Rogers announced that it would sell Freedom to Quebecor for $2.85 billion.

True Crime Byline: How one childhood incident pushed Dawn Crey into a downward spiral from which she would never escape   The new podcast True Crime Byline looks back at The Vancouver Sun investigation into Vancouver's missing women, a case that eventually led to the arrest and conviction of Robert Pickton. This profile of missing woman Dawn Crey was part of a series of investigative stories published in 2001.

True Crime Byline: A Politician Murdered his Ex Wife. We Should Have Seen it Coming   Looking back on the murder of JoAnn Wilson today, what's most surprising is that we were surprised it happened at all.

Jane Macdougall: The Bookless Club and the social science of ice cream cones   If someone floats the idea of going for ice cream cones, you know you’ve taken the relationship to the next level. It has all the hallmarks of, say, going to a baseball game but condensed into, well, condensed into as long as it takes for an ice cream cone to melt.

New manager of Toronto Blue Jays is a familiar face to Vancouver Canadians fans   "You couldn’t find a better baseball man or frankly a better human being. Even if you’re not a Toronto Blue Jays fan, you’ll want John Schneider to succeed. He’s that kind of guy.” — C's president Andy Dunn
Canucks: Mikael Samuelsson lands back in a familiar role with a familiar team   There were 10 Swedish players on the ice this week at Vancouver Canucks development camp — plus three coaches.

Whitecaps make a splash in transfer window, add wingback Julian Gressel   Is Julian Gressel the playmaking wingback the Whitecaps need? He had 13 assists in 2021 with DC United; no Vancouver Whitecaps player had more than five.

Artists were really happy to come back and I was really happy that pretty much all the artists I asked said yes, as long as they had the availability.” — Debbie Salmonsen, artistic and executive director of the Vancouver Folk Music Festival

'Beachcombers' star Pat John dead at 69   John joined the cast of The Beachcombers as a teenager, with the show debuting in 1972 and running for 19 seasons on CBC.

How Christian Bale became one of the great superhero movie villains   “It is a great joy to play villainous characters,” Bale told The Washington Post.

Anthony Gismondi: Top B.C. sparklers take honours at 2022 National Wine Awards   Number 1 in B.C.'s sparkling wines this year was the Silhouette N/V Boyd Classic Cuvee. The Boyd wines are made by the hardworking, highly talented winemaker Jacq Kemp.

Healthy Eating: Happy National Cherry Day   Cherry pits are generally safe to eat, even though they contain amygdalin, a chemical that the body can convert into cyanide.
No, Drake was not arrested in Sweden on cannabis charges   Unconfirmed reports on social media indicated the Grammy winner had been arrested at a Stockholm nightclub.

American Israeli woman formerly jailed in Russia over cannabis talks about Brittney Griner's case   Naama Issachar had about nine grams of cannabis in her possession and recalls feeling her heart sink when a Russian airport official discovered a small plastic bag tucked inside a fanny pack.
